title: RSF and SPLM-N Capture Kurmuk; Roseires Dam and Ed Damazin Threatened in Blue Nile Offensive
detail: A joint force of RSF fighters and Sudan People's Liberation Movement-North (SPLM-N, Abdul Aziz al-Hilu faction) captured Kurmuk, a strategic town on the Ethiopian border in Blue Nile State. Approximately 3,000 fighters staged from Ethiopia's Benishangul-Gumuz region (Asosa and Dul areas), with Sudan formally accusing Ethiopia of facilitating the attack. The SAF's 16th Infantry Brigade commander confirmed SAF withdrawal from the town. At least 30+ civilians were killed in fighting and shelling, and the Sudan Doctors Network reported the destruction of Kurmuk's hospital and pharmacy. Over 73,000 people fled toward Ed Damazin. The fall of Kurmuk opened the path toward the Geissan district and threatened Ed Damazin, the state capital, and the Roseires Dam — Sudan's second-largest hydroelectric facility. The RSF-SPLM-N alliance marked a significant expansion of the conflict's geographic scope.

media.caption: RSF and SPLM-N capture Kurmuk, threatening Roseires Dam and Ed Damazin

title: SAF Drones Strike Lagawa, West Kordofan; At Least 15 Killed, 23 Wounded in Residential Areas
detail: SAF drones struck residential neighborhoods in Lagawa, a town in West Kordofan under RSF control, killing at least 15 people and wounding 23. The RSF reported the toll as 17 dead and 25 injured. An eyewitness reported thick smoke rising from several neighborhoods in central Lagawa in the early morning hours. The RSF accused the SAF of 'systematic shelling of hospitals, markets, and residential neighbourhoods,' drawing condemnation from humanitarian organizations alongside the Al Daein hospital strike three days earlier. The Lagawa strike was one of multiple SAF drone attacks on civilian-populated RSF-held towns in Kordofan in late March 2026.

media.caption: SAF drones strike Lagawa, West Kordofan killing at least 15

title: Chadian Forces Shoot Civilians at Um Dukhun Border; 5 Killed, 14 Injured on Eid al-Fitr
detail: Chadian troops opened fire on civilians gathered near the Sudan-Chad border in Um Dukhun, Central Darfur, on Eid al-Fitr, killing 5 people and injuring 14. Eyewitnesses described families from both sides of the border celebrating the holiday with traditional dances when Chadian soldiers first attempted to disperse the crowd, then fired on people as they fled. Chadian forces reportedly pursued fleeing individuals up to 10 km inside Sudanese territory. The Chadian military said the operation targeted smugglers. Dozens of people were arrested and their fate remained unknown. The incident added to Chad-Sudan border tensions following Chad's closure of the 1,300 km border after an RSF drone killed 17 Chadians in Tine on March 18.

media.caption: Chadian forces shoot civilians at Um Dukhun border crossing
